element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Established in early levels before hazard complexity increases
Core damage system introduced early; Slick loses limbs on contact rather than traditional health
Non-enemy hazards teach avoidance patterns alongside enemy encounters
Resource collection mechanic paired with damage system to manage health recovery
Pattern-based encounters introduce boss mechanics before final confrontation
Culmination of all learned patterns; represents peak mechanical complexity

typical_tension_triggers: Increased obstacle density in later areas
Cheap hits from enemies (noted in reviews as frustration point)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty with wave-based tension: early areas establish mechanics safely, mid-areas increase hazard density and introduce Junkbot patterns, final area combines all elements for climactic Junkman confrontation. Reviews indicate frustration with 'mandatory cheap hits' suggesting uneven pacing.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational mechanics introduction (movement, collision system, resource collection) with moderate enemy/hazard pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased obstacle density and Junkbot encounters establish pattern recognition and boss combat fundamentals across Areas 2-4
difficulty_curve.late_game: Area 5 culminates with Junkman final boss combining all learned mechanics at peak difficulty
notable_spikes: First Junkbot midlevel boss (introduces pattern-based combat)
Junkman final boss (combines limb management, hazard avoidance, and boss patterns)
Enemy 'cheap hits' throughout (noted as difficulty frustration point in reviews)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Five-Area Structure with Midlevel Boss Gates
notable_level_patterns.description: Game divides into 5 distinct areas, each culminating in a Junkbot boss encounter that must be defeated to progress. This creates clear pacing breakpoints and skill gates.

Rather than traditional health bar, Slick loses individual limbs on enemy/hazard collision. Head falls off after all limbs are lost, causing death. Screwdrivers restore limbs. Creates distinctive resource management rhythm.
Loosely based on 1993 FOX Kids animated special; player controls Slick on rescue mission to save Dr. Zub from Junkman, providing narrative motivation for area progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Story arc spans Areas 1-5 to Junkman confrontation
